title: That God alone must be worshipped.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: That God alone must be worshipped.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: “As it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and Him only shalt thou serve.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Also in Exodus: “Thou shalt have none other gods beside me.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: Also in Deuteronomy: “See ye, see ye that I am He, and that there is no God beside me.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: I will kill, and will make alive; I will smite, and I will heal; and there is none who can deliver out of mine hands.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: In the Apocalypse, moreover: “And I saw another angel fly in the midst of heaven, having the everlasting Gospel to preach over the earth, and over all nations, and tribes, and tongues, and peoples, saying with a loud voice, Fear God rather, and give glory to Him: for the hour of His judgment is come; and worship Him that made heaven and earth, and the sea, and all that therein is.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: So also the Lord, in His Gospel, makes mention of the first and second commandment, saying, “Hear, O Israel, The Lord thy God is one God;” and, “Thou shalt love thy Lord with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: This is the first; and the second is like unto it, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets.”

verses.book: Exhortation to Martyrdom, Addressed to Fortunatus.
verses.text: And once more: “And this is life eternal, that they may know Thee, the only and true God, and Jesus Christ, whom Thou hast sent.”
